metrics.claim_path: no-llm/no-network (structural; the fast claim check is a local HMAC verify)

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add a concrete proving command to AC6: specify the test module path and `pytest` invocation (e.g., `pytest src/rebar/llm/code_review/tests/test_local_emit.py -v`) plus at minimum one `assert` pattern showing how the session-keyed artifact title and sidecar emission are verified.
findings.finding: No concrete proving command or test-file path is given for any acceptance criterion. AC6 names three test SCENARIOS (session-keyed run, bare-uuid run, artifact-failure swallowing) but does not specify how to execute them (no `pytest src/…` invocation, no assertion pattern, no test module path). An executor cannot tell whether these are new unit tests, integration tests, or a manual smoke-check, and cannot verify they pass CI without this.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria, AC6 (Tests bullet)

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add an end-to-end AC (or extend AC1) with a concrete live proving command: run `REBAR_SESSION_ID=test-sess rebar review-code` against a minimal diff, then `rebar list_tickets --type code_review | jq -e '.[] | select(.title == "code-review: session:test-sess")'` to confirm the artifact was created in the real ticket store.
findings.finding: The new local-emit code path (the cutover from 'emits nothing' to 'emits a session-keyed artifact') has no described end-to-end live check. All verification is at the unit/mock level implied by AC6. No AC exercises the actual `rebar review-code` CLI against a real diff and confirms the artifact appears in `rebar list_tickets` — the only way to confirm the live production path works.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria, AC1 and AC6; What section 'Wire the local gate entry to emit + persist'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add the relevant test file(s) (e.g. `tests/llm/test_local_code_review_artifact.py`) to the Scope section so AC6 has a corresponding described deliverable.
findings.finding: AC6 (tests) names three specific test scenarios but no test file or test module appears anywhere in the Scope section, making AC6 an orphan criterion with no described deliverable to verify against.
findings.location: Scope section (file list) vs AC6
findings.suggested_fix: Add to Scope: `tests/llm/test_local_code_review_artifact.py` (or the appropriate existing test module) covering the three AC6 scenarios: session-keyed emit/reuse across two runs, uuid fallback on bare invocation, and best-effort swallowing of artifact failures.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Clarify AC1 to specify which identifier is `session:<id>` — e.g. 'artifact title `code-review: session:{id}`; top-level `session_id` field value `{id}`; typed memory key `session:{id}`' — so the AC is unambiguous about what is being verified.
findings.finding: AC1 describes the artifact as 'keyed `session:<id>`', while the What section defines two distinct identifiers: the artifact title `code-review: session:{session_key}` and the typed memory key `session:<session_key>`. An implementer verifying AC1 cannot tell which string `session:<id>` refers to.
findings.location: AC1 vs What section ('Set `session_id` on the verdict' and 'Create-or-reuse idempotency' paragraphs)
findings.suggested_fix: Rewrite AC1 as: 'Local `rebar review-code` emits a `code_review` artifact with title `code-review: session:{id}`, a top-level `session_id` field equal to `{id}`, and a typed memory key of `session:{id}`, carrying the reviewed-file `deps` map.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Acknowledge the duplication risk of mirroring voter.emit_code_review_artifact in gate_dispatch.py, and either extract a shared helper (e.g. `_emit_session_artifact`) usable by both paths, or explicitly document the duplication as an accepted trade-off with a note to keep them in sync.
findings.finding: The local-emit helper in gate_dispatch.py is specified to mirror voter.emit_code_review_artifact (voter.py ~199–220) 'EXACTLY,' duplicating artifact create-or-reuse logic across two files with no shared abstraction or extraction planned. A future change to the emit pattern (title format, list_tickets filter, sidecar call signature) must be applied in both places, and there is no plan acknowledgement of this coupling risk or a mitigation strategy.
findings.location: What section — 'Wire the local gate entry to emit + persist' / 'Create-or-reuse idempotency'; Scope — gate_dispatch.py
findings.suggested_fix: Extract the create-or-reuse + sidecar emit logic into a shared helper in a common module (e.g. `rebar/llm/code_review/artifact_emit.py`) and have both voter.py and the new local path call it. If extraction is out of scope, add a comment in both files cross-referencing the twin and a TODO to consolidate.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add a brief ADR or CLAUDE.md note capturing: (a) why session-keyed artifact titles are the idempotency anchor, (b) why the uuid4 fallback is intentionally inert (no cross-run convergence for bare invocations), and (c) how local and Gerrit emit paths relate (parallel patterns, not shared helper — or shared helper if extracted).
findings.finding: The plan introduces a novel architectural pattern — local `review-code` now emits session-keyed artifacts with create-or-reuse idempotency — but includes no plan to document this decision in an ADR, CLAUDE.md, or design doc. Future contributors have no durable record of why the session-keyed title convention was chosen, why the uuid4 fallback is intentionally inert, or how local and Gerrit emit paths relate.
findings.location: Why / What / Scope sections; AC1–AC4

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Address the TOCTOU race in create-or-reuse: specify an atomic or idempotent create strategy (e.g., accept-duplicate-then-deduplicate-on-read, advisory lock, or treat a title-collision error from the ticket store as 'already exists' and re-scan) so AC4 holds under concurrent subagent invocations sharing a session key.
findings.finding: The create-or-reuse logic (scan for title match → if absent → create) is a check-then-act TOCTOU race: two subagents sharing the same REBAR_SESSION_ID can both find no existing artifact and both call create_ticket, producing duplicate session-keyed artifacts and violating AC4's 'same artifact' guarantee.
findings.location: What > Wire the local gate entry to emit + persist > Create-or-reuse idempotency; AC4
findings.suggested_fix: On a create_ticket collision (duplicate title error from the store), catch the error, re-scan for the now-existing artifact, and return its id — converting the race into a harmless retry. Document this in the plan so the implementer handles the error branch explicitly.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Define a RETIRE/cleanup path for session-keyed code_review artifacts (e.g., TTL policy, max-events-per-artifact cap, or an explicit note that lifecycle management is deferred to a future story) so the shared-state lifecycle is complete.
findings.finding: No RETIRE or cleanup path is defined for session-keyed code_review artifacts; they accumulate indefinitely in the ticket store as sessions come and go, with no TTL, archival, or pruning strategy.
findings.location: What > Wire the local gate entry to emit + persist; Scope section

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Specify the minimum observability for artifact-path failures: at minimum a structured WARNING-level log entry with session_key, operation (create/reuse/emit/link), and exception type — or explicitly state that the existing voter/sidecar log schema is reused verbatim.
findings.finding: Observability of artifact-path failures is under-specified: the plan says failures are 'logged' but does not define the log level, structured fields, or whether any metric/counter is emitted, making it difficult for operators to detect silent persistence failures in production.
findings.location: What > Wire the local gate entry to emit + persist > Best-effort throughout
findings.suggested_fix: Mirror voter.py's existing log call signature (level, fields) explicitly in the plan so the implementer doesn't have to infer it.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Clarify (in the Scope section) whether an externally-supplied `target_ticket` to `shim.review_code` acts as an override that skips the local create-or-reuse scan, or is always ignored in the local path (with create-or-reuse always authoritative), so AC4's idempotency guarantee is unambiguous.
findings.finding: The plan adds `target_ticket: str | None = None` to `shim.review_code` (and AC3 confirms it is forwarded) but never specifies whether gate_dispatch's local-emit helper treats an externally-supplied `target_ticket` as an override that skips the create-or-reuse scan, or ignores it in favour of the internally-computed `artifact_id`. The interaction is unspecified, leaving the implementor to guess — risking either a silent double-emit (external ticket ignored) or bypassing the idempotency guarantee (create-or-reuse skipped).
findings.location: Scope / 'Signature changes (in scope)' bullet; AC3
findings.suggested_fix: Add one sentence: 'In the local path, `target_ticket` passed to `shim.review_code` is forwarded to `CodeReviewRequest` but is treated as an advisory hint only; the local-emit helper always performs the create-or-reuse scan and uses the resolved `artifact_id` for `sidecar.emit`, ensuring AC4 idempotency regardless of any externally-supplied value.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Instead of duplicating the create-or-reuse artifact logic in gate_dispatch.py, refactor `voter.emit_code_review_artifact` (or extract a shared helper in `code_review/sidecar.py`) to accept a caller-supplied `title` and optional `change_id`/`revision`, so both the Gerrit and local paths call ONE implementation with different title arguments.
findings.finding: The plan proposes a new 'local-emit helper' in gate_dispatch.py that mirrors voter.emit_code_review_artifact 'EXACTLY' — duplicating the list_tickets title-scan, create_ticket on miss, sidecar.emit, and relates_to linking logic — when the only structural difference from the existing function is the title formula. This is NIH duplication of ~50 lines already in the codebase.
findings.location: What section — 'Create-or-reuse idempotency' bullet; also 'Scope' entry for gate_dispatch.py; AC4
findings.suggested_fix: Extract the create-or-reuse core into a function in `src/rebar/llm/code_review/sidecar.py`, e.g. `emit_artifact(title, verdict, *, repo_root, commit_message='', change_id='', revision='', change_fp='')`, that does the list_tickets scan, create_ticket, sidecar.emit, and relates_to linking. Then have voter.emit_code_review_artifact and the new local path both call it with their respective title strings. This eliminates the duplication with no new abstraction layer.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Remove or qualify the 'validated shared-across-subagents, unique-per-parallel-session' parenthetical on `resolve_session_id()` in the plan — the resolver does zero validation (it is a plain env-var lookup); any isolation guarantee is a harness-level property, and the plan should say so explicitly rather than implying the function enforces it.
findings.finding: The plan parenthetically asserts that `resolve_session_id()` is 'validated shared-across-subagents, unique-per-parallel-session', implying the function enforces or verifies these session identity properties. Reading `src/rebar/_commands/session_id.py` shows the function is simply `_first_nonempty(*_SESSION_ID_VARS)` — a plain env-var lookup with no validation, uniqueness check, or cross-subagent sharing verification. The 'validated' framing is ungrounded by the implementation.
findings.location: ## What — Resolve the local memory key: '(precedence: … validated shared-across-subagents, unique-per-parallel-session)'
findings.suggested_fix: Replace '(precedence: … ; validated shared-across-subagents, unique-per-parallel-session)' with '(precedence: … ; these properties are harness/shim guarantees — the resolver reads env vars verbatim and enforces nothing)' to accurately reflect what `resolve_session_id()` does.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Clarify AC3 and the 'Signature changes' paragraph: `target_ticket` already exists on `CodeReviewRequest`; only `session_id: str | None = None` is a new field to add to the dataclass.
findings.finding: `CodeReviewRequest` in `gate_dispatch.py` already has a `target_ticket: str | None = None` field (line 467); the plan's AC3 and 'What / Signature changes' section say to ADD it, describing work that is already done and will produce a conflict or confusion during implementation.
findings.location: What > Wire the local gate entry > Signature changes (in scope); AC3; src/rebar/llm/workflow/gate_dispatch.py line 467
findings.suggested_fix: Update AC3 to read: '`shim.review_code` gains a `session_id` parameter (and already accepts `target_ticket` via `CodeReviewRequest`); `CodeReviewRequest` gains a `session_id: str | None = None` field.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add `src/rebar/_mcp_llm.py` to the out-of-scope section (or in-scope) with an explicit decision on whether the MCP `review_code` tool should also resolve and thread the session key.
findings.finding: The MCP `review_code` tool in `src/rebar/_mcp_llm.py` calls `rebar.llm.review_code(base=…, head=…, reviewers=…, ref=…, source=…)` without passing `session_id` or `target_ticket`, so it will silently omit session-keyed artifact emission when called via MCP. This caller is not mentioned in the plan's scope or out-of-scope sections.
findings.location: Scope section; src/rebar/_mcp_llm.py lines 88-90

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Explicitly state the c639 interface contract for `build_payload`: does it read `verdict.get('session_id')` from the verdict dict (requiring only that the verdict dict carry the key), or does it add a `session_id=` keyword parameter? Confirm or specify which approach revenued-thickset-dassie delivers.
findings.finding: The plan states `build_payload` (in `code_review/sidecar.py`) 'reads `verdict.get("session_id")`' and carries it top-level — but the CURRENT `build_payload` does not read `session_id` at all. The plan attributes this to the c639 / revenued-thickset-dassie dependency, but never specifies that `build_payload`'s SIGNATURE also needs a change (it currently takes only `target_ticket`, `change_id`, `revision`, `change_fp`). The emit helper in `gate_dispatch.py` will call `sidecar.emit(verdict, …)` — if `build_payload` just reads `verdict.get('session_id')` from the dict, no signature change is needed, but if it needs to expose `session_id` as a standalone payload key, the caller contract must be clarified.
findings.location: What > Set `session_id` on the verdict; Scope > 'Depends on the payload shape + reader (revenued-thickset-dassie)'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Clarify why the local-emit helper belongs in `gate_dispatch.py` rather than in `shim.py` (which already receives the verdict and could accept `session_id` directly, mirroring how `voter.py` emits without touching `gate_dispatch`) — or restructure the emit call to live in `shim.py` after `produce_code_review_verdict` returns, keeping `gate_dispatch` a pure dispatch module.
findings.finding: The plan places the local-emit helper (artifact lifecycle: list/create/emit/link) inside gate_dispatch.py, but gate_dispatch.py is explicitly scoped to gate-engine verdict production; the analogous Gerrit voter path emits from voter.py (outside gate_dispatch), not from inside the dispatcher. Adding artifact-lifecycle responsibility to gate_dispatch adds a new cross-cutting concern to the dispatch module, increasing blast radius on a complex module, while the plan's own 'mirroring voter.py' rationale actually supports emitting from the shim or a dedicated local helper instead. The plan provides no positive rationale for WHY gate_dispatch is the right emission boundary over the shim.
findings.location: Scope section / 'Wire the local gate entry to emit + persist' / gate_dispatch.py local-emit helper
findings.suggested_fix: Move the local-emit logic to `shim.py::review_code`: after `produce_code_review_verdict` returns the verdict, call a `_emit_local_session_artifact(verdict, session_id=session_key, commit_message=commit_message, repo_root=repo_root)` helper defined in `shim.py` (or a new `code_review/local_artifact.py`). `shim.py` already has the verdict, session_key, and commit_message needed for the `relates_to` link. `CodeReviewRequest` then does NOT need a `session_id` field, keeping the frozen dataclass narrowly scoped to gate-dispatch inputs. This mirrors the voter/gate_dispatch split exactly.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Correct the Scope section to state that only `session_id: str | None = None` is a NEW addition to `CodeReviewRequest`; `target_ticket` already exists and must NOT be re-added (gate_dispatch.py line 467).
findings.finding: The plan's Scope section instructs adding `target_ticket: str | None = None` to `CodeReviewRequest` in gate_dispatch.py, but CodeReviewRequest already has that field (gate_dispatch.py line 467). Attempting to add a duplicate field to a frozen dataclass will raise a TypeError at definition time.
findings.location: Scope section: 'add `target_ticket: str | None = None` and `session_id: str | None = None` parameters … AND add a `session_id: str | None = None` field to the `CodeReviewRequest` dataclass'
findings.suggested_fix: In the Scope bullet, change 'add `target_ticket: str | None = None` and `session_id: str | None = None` … to `CodeReviewRequest`' to 'add `session_id: str | None = None` to `CodeReviewRequest` (alongside the existing `target_ticket` field, which is already present)'.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add an explicit implementation-order guard: note in the plan (or as a test assertion) that `build_payload` must include a `session_id` key in its output before this story can function end-to-end, so the implementer verifies c639 is merged before wiring the local emit path.
findings.finding: The plan asserts `build_payload` in `sidecar.py` 'reads `verdict.get("session_id")`' as an already-available capability from c639, but the currently-readable `sidecar.py` does not contain `session_id` in `build_payload`. The plan correctly gates this story on c639 committing first, but does not state an explicit integration-readiness check or fallback if c639's `build_payload` extension is absent at implementation time.
findings.location: What section: 'Set `session_id` on the verdict … the payload (revenued-thickset-dassie's `build_payload`, which reads `verdict.get("session_id")`) carries it top-level'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Specify how the HEAD commit message is obtained on the local path for the `relates_to` link: either (a) read it in `_llm_commands.py` via `git log -1 --format=%B HEAD` and thread it through `shim.review_code` → `CodeReviewRequest` → the local-emit helper, or (b) have the local-emit helper itself call git, and add this to the Scope description for `_llm_commands.py` or `gate_dispatch.py`.
findings.finding: The plan never specifies how the head commit message is obtained for the local `relates_to` link: the `_review_code` CLI function does not pass `commit_message` to `review_code()`, `shim.review_code` receives no commit_message for the local-emit path, and no git-read call is described to fetch it.
findings.location: What section: 'relates_to link (best-effort)' / Scope: `src/rebar/_cli/_llm_commands.py` / AC5

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Specify the exact placement of (a) `verdict["session_id"] = request.session_id` (must be BEFORE any sidecar.emit call in `_run_code_review_gate`) and (b) whether the new local-emit helper is invoked from inside `_run_code_review_gate` as a replacement for the existing `if request.target_ticket:` block, or as a separate code path that the existing block delegates to.
findings.finding: The decision rule for where `verdict["session_id"] = session_key` is set relative to the existing `if request.target_ticket: sidecar.emit(...)` block in `_run_code_review_gate` is ambiguous: the plan describes a 'local-emit helper' in gate_dispatch.py but does not specify whether it replaces, wraps, or supplements the current emit block, leaving the sequencing of the session_id stamp and the emit call unresolved.
findings.location: What section: 'Wire the local gate entry to emit + persist' / Scope: `src/rebar/llm/workflow/gate_dispatch.py`
findings.suggested_fix: Have the local-emit helper called from within `_run_code_review_gate` after `_detectors.apply_failclosed` and after `verdict["session_id"] = request.session_id` is set, replacing the bare `if request.target_ticket: sidecar.emit(...)` block with a unified `_local_emit_session_artifact(verdict, request)` that handles both create-or-reuse and the sidecar emit.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add a decision rule specifying the precedence between an externally-supplied `target_ticket` and the locally-resolved session artifact_id — e.g., 'the local-emit helper only runs when `request.target_ticket` is None; if target_ticket is already set, the existing sidecar.emit path handles it unchanged'.
findings.finding: When an external `target_ticket` is supplied to `shim.review_code` (AC3 adds this parameter) AND the local-emit helper would also resolve its own session-keyed artifact_id, the plan defines no precedence rule for which artifact the sidecar event is emitted to — it is silent on whether they co-emit, or one takes priority.
findings.location: What section: 'Signature changes (in scope)' / AC3 / Scope: `src/rebar/llm/code_review/shim.py`
findings.suggested_fix: State explicitly: 'the local-emit helper fires only when `request.target_ticket is None`; when `request.target_ticket` is set (e.g. by the Gerrit adapter), the existing emit path is used unchanged and no session artifact is created.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Name the test file(s) in the Scope section that cover AC6's three scenarios (e.g. `tests/unit/test_code_review_artifact.py` for artifact reuse, or a new `tests/unit/test_local_code_review_emit.py`), matching the convention used in sibling stories.
findings.finding: The test file for the new local-emit behavior is absent from the Scope section: AC6 enumerates three specific test scenarios (session-keyed artifact reuse across two runs, uuid-key bare invocation, artifact failures don't fail review) but no test module is named in the Scope, leaving the test deliverable as an orphan criterion.
findings.location: Scope section / AC6

metrics.claim_path: no-llm/no-network (structural; the fast claim check is a local HMAC verify)
